Details:
Potassium permanganate (KMnO4) is an oxidizing agent with a strong affinity for organic compounds containing carbon-carbon double bonds, aldehyde groups or hydroxyl groups. Although the chemical structure of RDX does not make it an ideal candidate for attack by permanganate, effective RDX loss by treatment  with KMnO4 has been demonstrated. [1730] Adams et al show 97% removal of RDX using KMnO4 within 11 days. Another bench-scale study found RDX removals of 72 to 100% using high concentrations of KMnO4 [1733]. However, the study showed that temperature strongly influenced the reaction kinetics, with a three-fold decrease in the reaction rate at 11.5 degrees C versus 23 degrees C.
Permanganate doses and contact times were extreme for drinking water treatment conditions.
